protagonist.Name: Professor Adrian Holt
protagonist.Reason: He is a professor of linguistics and ancient history who won a week-long, all-expenses-paid stay at the lodge in a national cryptography competition. He is a widower and has come with his teenage daughter who's a genius in her own right. He genuinely believes the old lodge holds a historical cipher he hopes to crack.
protagonist.Connection: His analytical mind and obsession with solving complex puzzles mean he is likely to be the first to notice inconsistencies or hidden patterns among the guests, making him an accidental investigator. He is completely unknown to the other guests.

characters.Name: Willow Holt
characters.Reason: She is accompanying her father, Professor Holt, on his prize trip. Willow is a high school student who excels in applied mathematics, coding, and digital forensics. While her father focuses on historical ciphers, she uses modern tech to solve puzzles. She is largely indifferent to the resort's social aspects, preferring to be on her laptop.
characters.Connection: She is the Professor's dedicated, though sometimes exasperated, assistant. Her modern technological skills complement her father's old-world academic knowledge, providing the means to process and analyze data (like security footage or communications intercepts) that her father's methods can't touch.

characters.Name: Elara Vance
characters.Reason: She inherited the 'Peak Vista Lodge' from her eccentric uncle last year and is determined to make its centennial season a success, despite having no prior hospitality experience. She feels the weight of her family's legacy.
characters.Connection: She is the host and manager of the resort where everyone is trapped.

characters.Name: Marcus 'Mac' Chen
characters.Reason: He booked the trip under a pseudonym, seeking isolation and a break from the media frenzy following a career-ending professional mistake. He planned to write a book exposing a hidden corporate scandal.
characters.Connection: He is secretly trailing Sebastian Reed (Character 9) and believes a key source of information is also staying at the lodge.

characters.Name: Dr. Evelyn Shaw
characters.Reason: She is a trauma surgeon who was skiing nearby and was stranded on her way back to the city for a critical conference. She volunteered her services when the blizzard hit and realized the resort had limited medical staff.
characters.Connection: She is the estranged older sister of Liam Shaw (Character 7).

characters.Name: Silas Thorne
characters.Reason: He owns a substantial share in the corporation that funds the Peak Vista Lodge, and is there for an unscheduled, secretive 'inspection' to assess if the property is worth selling off after the season.
characters.Connection: He is the primary financial backer (and potential executioner) of Elara Vance's (Character 1) resort. He is also a former business partner of Octavia Sterling (Character 10).

characters.Name: Jasmine Khan
characters.Reason: She came to the remote resort seeking inspiration and anonymity after a high-profile breakup and a sudden, unwelcome burst of fame that she finds terrifying. She is trying to finish a commissioned painting.
characters.Connection: She is the fiancée of Leo Bellwether (Character 6), though their relationship is currently on a secret 'pause.'

characters.Name: Leo Bellwether
characters.Reason: He was hired by a nervous client to discreetly install a new, state-of-the-art security system at the resort, which must be completed before the blizzard lifts. He uses this technical job as an excuse to clear his head about his relationship.
characters.Connection: He is the fiancé of Jasmine Khan (Character 5). He is also the one who installed the surveillance system that Marcus Chen (Character 2) is trying to access.

characters.Name: Liam Shaw
characters.Reason: He works year-round at the resort, having taken the job to escape the high-pressure fine-dining world and be closer to the mountains. He is deeply protective of the staff.
characters.Connection: He is the younger brother of Dr. Evelyn Shaw (Character 3). He is secretly in love with Elara Vance (Character 1).

characters.Name: Penelope 'Penny' Hayes
characters.Reason: She rented a cabin outside the main lodge to conduct interviews for a new series focusing on missing persons in the Colorado mountains. She needs to interview a key witness who was expected to arrive via snowmobile.
characters.Connection: Her current research subject involves a cold case related to a hiker who disappeared on land owned by Silas Thorne (Character 4).

characters.Name: Sebastian Reed
characters.Reason: He is meeting a contact to exchange highly sensitive encrypted files concerning a pharmaceutical company's illegal trials. He is paranoid and constantly looking over his shoulder.
characters.Connection: He is the subject of Marcus Chen's (Character 2) investigative book and is terrified that his former associate, Octavia Sterling (Character 10), is trying to locate him.

characters.Name: Octavia Sterling
characters.Reason: She claims to be a landscape architect scouting locations, but she is actually working for a shadowy corporate firm. Her real mission is to locate and neutralize Sebastian Reed (Character 9) and retrieve the stolen files.
characters.Connection: She is the former business associate of Silas Thorne (Character 4) and is actively hunting Sebastian Reed (Character 9).

setting.location: Peak Vista Lodge an Exclusive Ski Resort in Colorado - Snowed in due to Blizzard - No phone signal

hidden_truth.motive: To prevent investigative journalist Arthur Finch from publishing an expose on her past medical malpractice, which had ruined his family and threatened her career.
hidden_truth.method: Dr. Shaw subtly administered a rare, slow-acting paralytic agent into Arthur Finch's evening herbal tea, which he regularly ordered via room service. The agent was designed to mimic a heart attack or natural causes, making his death appear unsuspicious. The blizzard conditions and lack of external communication provided the perfect cover for the crime.
hidden_truth.hidden_truth_summary: Dr. Evelyn Shaw murdered investigative journalist Arthur Finch to cover up her past medical malpractice. She poisoned his herbal tea with a slow-acting paralytic, ensuring his death appeared natural amidst the isolation of the blizzard-stricken lodge.

suspects.name: Dr. Evelyn Shaw
suspects.apparent_motive: None. She maintains a professional and helpful demeanor as the lodge's resident doctor.
suspects.alibi: Claimed she was in the lodge's small medical bay, reviewing patient files and preparing for potential emergencies given the blizzard. She stated she was there all evening.

suspects.name: Marcus 'Mac' Chen
suspects.apparent_motive: Arthur Finch had written a scathing article about Mac's failing tech startup, causing him significant financial losses and public humiliation just months prior.
suspects.alibi: Was in the main lounge, loudly arguing with a staff member about the lack of internet connection, then played cards with Leo Bellwether for several hours.

suspects.name: Elara Vance
suspects.apparent_motive: Finch had been investigating Elara's prominent charity foundation, suspecting embezzlement, and was reportedly close to publishing a damaging report.
suspects.alibi: Claimed she was meditating alone in her private suite all evening, stating she heard nothing unusual and saw no one.

suspects.name: Silas Thorne
suspects.apparent_motive: Appears to have no direct connection to Finch. However, he is a reclusive former intelligence operative who was discreetly investigating Dr. Shaw for an unrelated case, and might have been trying to access Finch's research.
suspects.alibi: Claimed to be in the lodge's library, reading through old historical journals. The librarian on duty confirms he was present for most of the evening.

suspects.name: Liam Shaw
suspects.apparent_motive: Dr. Evelyn Shaw's younger brother. He is fiercely protective of his sister and her reputation, and may have known about Finch's investigation, leading him to act to protect her.
suspects.alibi: Claimed he was in his staff quarters, playing video games with Penny Hayes for several hours. Penny Hayes corroborates his story.

locations: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

dire_fate: Dr. Evelyn Shaw will escape justice, and her past medical malpractice will remain hidden, allowing her to continue her career unchecked.

actions_taken.action: Investigate the Medical Bay's inventory and Dr. Shaw's recent prescriptions, looking for any unusual or missing substances.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: Dr. Shaw's medical bay is impeccably organized, but the inventory logs for controlled substances are digital and require a specific access code, which she 'forgot' and is unavailable until morning due to a system update. | Discovered: While unable to access the digital logs, the detective notices a small, unlabeled vial tucked away in a back cabinet, separate from the labeled medications, with no clear purpose. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: Interview the lodge staff responsible for room service in the Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area, specifically about Arthur Finch's herbal tea delivery on the night of his death.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: The room service attendant, Chloe, recalls that Dr. Shaw personally insisted on preparing and delivering a 'special blend' of herbal tea to Finch's room that evening, bypassing standard room service procedures. Chloe notes Dr. Shaw seemed unusually focused on this task, even for a doctor concerned about a guest's well-being. | Discovered: Dr. Shaw bypassed standard protocol to personally prepare and deliver a 'special blend' of tea to Finch, providing a direct opportunity for her to administer poison. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: The Detective attempts to discreetly retrieve the unlabeled vial from the Medical Bay for forensic analysis.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: Dr. Shaw, having observed the detective's previous interest in the medical bay, has already secured the vial. She claims it contained a personal experimental blend of essential oils for aromatherapy, which she moved to her private quarters to prevent accidental misuse. | Discovered: While the vial is gone, the detective notices a barely perceptible residue on the shelf where it sat, which seems to shimmer faintly under a UV light, indicating a possible chemical trace not consistent with essential oils. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: The Detective reviews security footage (if any) or interviews staff who might have seen Dr. Shaw or Liam Shaw near the medical bay or Finch's suite.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: The lodge's archaic security system only records footage from the main entrances and lounge, not the corridors leading to the medical bay or guest suites. However, a night porter recalls seeing Liam Shaw exiting Finch's suite (Room 307) late on the night of the murder, looking agitated, before quickly heading towards the staff quarters. The porter assumed it was a routine staff check. | Discovered: Liam Shaw was seen leaving Finch's suite late on the night of the murder, looking agitated. This contradicts his alibi of being in staff quarters playing games and directly places him at the scene. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: Re-interview Chloe, the room service attendant, with specific questions about the 'exotic herbs' Dr. Shaw used in Finch's tea, asking if she recognized them or if they had any unusual effects.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: Chloe recalls that the herbs had a very faint, almost imperceptible, bitter aftertaste, which she dismissed at the time as just part of the "exotic" blend. She also remembers Dr. Shaw specifically telling her to make sure Finch drank *all* of the tea for maximum benefit. | Discovered: The tea had a faint bitter aftertaste, and Dr. Shaw insisted Finch drink all of it. This strongly suggests a hidden substance within the tea, and Dr. Shaw's insistence points to a desired dosage. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: The Detective conducts a thorough, discreet search of the Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area, focusing on waste bins, discarded containers, or hidden stashes that might relate to Dr. Shaw's 'special blend' tea.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: The kitchen staff maintains strict cleanliness protocols, and the area was thoroughly cleaned after the incident. However, tucked away in a rarely used dry storage cabinet, the detective finds a small, unlabeled, empty bottle of a rare, imported herbal supplement, known for its calming properties... and in large doses, its potential to cause muscle weakness and paralysis. The bottle's label is in a foreign language, but a small, smudged sticker indicates it was ordered by "Dr. E. Shaw." | Discovered: An empty bottle of a rare, imported herbal supplement, capable of causing muscle weakness and paralysis in large doses, was found in a hidden cabinet in the kitchen. A smudged sticker links its order to 'Dr. E. Shaw'. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: The Detective attempts to research the rare, imported herbal supplement found in the kitchen, specifically looking into its origins, typical uses, and known distributors, to understand how Dr. Shaw might have acquired it.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: The supplement is indeed rare, sourced from a remote, specialized clinic known for experimental holistic treatments in a country with lax regulations. The clinic's records are highly private, but a discreet inquiry reveals Dr. Shaw has been a regular, high-volume client for several months, often ordering 'research samples' for her 'personal studies.' This suggests premeditation. | Discovered: Dr. Shaw has been regularly ordering this rare, potentially paralytic supplement for months, indicating premeditation and a deeper, hidden agenda related to experimental substances. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: The Detective discreetly collects a sample of the shimmering residue from the medical bay shelf and arranges for an urgent off-site forensic analysis.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: The forensic lab reports a partial analysis, confirming the residue contains traces of a highly potent neurotoxin, chemically similar to the rare herbal supplement found. However, before a full identification can be made, the lab's equipment experiences a sudden, inexplicable malfunction, delaying further results and raising suspicions of sabotage. | Discovered: The residue confirms a potent neurotoxin, strongly linking it to the paralytic. The lab malfunction suggests active interference to obstruct the investigation. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: The Detective confronts Dr. Evelyn Shaw directly, presenting her with the empty bottle of the rare paralytic supplement found in the kitchen, and asking for an explanation regarding her repeated orders.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: Dr. Shaw maintains her composure, claiming the supplement was for her personal 'holistic research' into natural sleep aids and muscle relaxants, which she sometimes experimented with herself, keeping it in the kitchen for convenience. She then subtly reminds the detective of the lodge's strict privacy policies and threatens to report harassment. However, her hands tremble slightly, and she avoids direct eye contact when explaining its 'personal use'. | Discovered: Dr. Shaw's composure cracks slightly under pressure, revealing physical signs of stress (trembling hands) despite her verbal denial and threats. Her explanation for the supplement's presence is flimsy and suspicious. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: The Detective discreetly searches Dr. Evelyn Shaw's private quarters, specifically looking for the unlabeled vial she claimed to have moved there, or any other evidence related to the paralytic.
actions_taken.outcome: Problem: The vial is not present. However, hidden beneath a loose floorboard, the detective finds a small, waterproof pouch containing a burner phone and a handwritten ledger. The ledger details large, irregular payments to a private investigator for 'discretionary services' and mentions 'Finch's investigation' several times, along with references to 'old patient files' and 'suppressing information'. The burner phone shows recent calls to an untraceable number, and one outgoing message: 'Lab compromised. Proceed with contingency.' | Discovered: A hidden burner phone and ledger in Dr. Shaw's quarters reveal a paid private investigator, a direct link to 'Finch's investigation' and 'old patient files', and a message confirming the lab sabotage, indicating a wider, premeditated cover-up. | Was Red Herring: False

actions_taken.action: The Detective confronts Dr. Evelyn Shaw again, this time presenting the evidence of the hidden ledger, the burner phone with the 'Lab compromised' message, and the details about the private investigator, demanding she explain the extent of her cover-up.
actions_taken.outcome: Goal Achieved! Resolution: Under intense pressure, Dr. Shaw's composure finally shatters. She confesses to hiring the private investigator to monitor Finch and to having Liam tamper with the lab, but she vehemently denies direct involvement in Finch's death, blaming a 'misunderstanding' of the paralytic's dosage by Liam, who she claims administered it without her direct instruction. She argues she only wanted to incapacitate Finch temporarily to retrieve his research, not kill him. However, her detailed knowledge of the paralytic's effects, her previous insistence that Finch drink *all* the tea, and her desperate attempt to shift blame to Liam, strongly implicate her as the mastermind behind the murder. | Discovered: Dr. Shaw confesses to hiring the PI and having Liam tamper with the lab, admitting to a cover-up. She attempts to blame Liam for administering the paralytic, claiming she only wanted to incapacitate Finch, but her deep knowledge of the substance and her desperate shifting of blame make her guilt evident.

story_elements.description: Investigate the Medical Bay's inventory and Dr. Shaw's recent prescriptions, looking for any unusual or missing substances.
story_elements.reasoning: If Finch was poisoned, a doctor (like Evelyn Shaw) would have access to such substances. Checking her inventory could reveal a potential weapon.

story_elements.description: Dr. Shaw's medical bay is impeccably organized, but the inventory logs for controlled substances are digital and require a specific access code, which she 'forgot' and is unavailable until morning due to a system update.
story_elements.new_info: While unable to access the digital logs, the detective notices a small, unlabeled vial tucked away in a back cabinet, separate from the labeled medications, with no clear purpose.

story_elements.description: Interview the lodge staff responsible for room service in the Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area, specifically about Arthur Finch's herbal tea delivery on the night of his death.
story_elements.reasoning: If Finch was poisoned via his tea, understanding its preparation and delivery is crucial. Lodge staff might have observed something unusual or noticed someone tampering with the tea.

story_elements.description: The room service attendant, Chloe, recalls that Dr. Shaw personally insisted on preparing and delivering a 'special blend' of herbal tea to Finch's room that evening, bypassing standard room service procedures. Chloe notes Dr. Shaw seemed unusually focused on this task, even for a doctor concerned about a guest's well-being.
story_elements.new_info: Dr. Shaw bypassed standard protocol to personally prepare and deliver a 'special blend' of tea to Finch, providing a direct opportunity for her to administer poison.

story_elements.description: The Detective attempts to discreetly retrieve the unlabeled vial from the Medical Bay for forensic analysis.
story_elements.reasoning: The hidden, unlabeled vial is highly suspicious, especially after learning Dr. Shaw personally handled Finch's tea. Analyzing its contents could reveal a direct link to his death.

story_elements.description: Dr. Shaw, having observed the detective's previous interest in the medical bay, has already secured the vial. She claims it contained a personal experimental blend of essential oils for aromatherapy, which she moved to her private quarters to prevent accidental misuse.
story_elements.new_info: While the vial is gone, the detective notices a barely perceptible residue on the shelf where it sat, which seems to shimmer faintly under a UV light, indicating a possible chemical trace not consistent with essential oils.

story_elements.description: The Detective reviews security footage (if any) or interviews staff who might have seen Dr. Shaw or Liam Shaw near the medical bay or Finch's suite.
story_elements.reasoning: Dr. Shaw's alibi is key, and Liam's suspicious presence near the medical bay is a new development that warrants further investigation.

story_elements.description: The lodge's archaic security system only records footage from the main entrances and lounge, not the corridors leading to the medical bay or guest suites. However, a night porter recalls seeing Liam Shaw exiting Finch's suite (Room 307) late on the night of the murder, looking agitated, before quickly heading towards the staff quarters. The porter assumed it was a routine staff check.
story_elements.new_info: Liam Shaw was seen leaving Finch's suite late on the night of the murder, looking agitated. This contradicts his alibi of being in staff quarters playing games and directly places him at the scene.

story_elements.description: Re-interview Chloe, the room service attendant, with specific questions about the 'exotic herbs' Dr. Shaw used in Finch's tea, asking if she recognized them or if they had any unusual effects.
story_elements.reasoning: Dr. Shaw's personal preparation and use of "exotic herbs" from an unmarked pouch is highly suspicious and could be the vehicle for the poison.

story_elements.description: Chloe recalls that the herbs had a very faint, almost imperceptible, bitter aftertaste, which she dismissed at the time as just part of the "exotic" blend. She also remembers Dr. Shaw specifically telling her to make sure Finch drank *all* of the tea for maximum benefit.
story_elements.new_info: The tea had a faint bitter aftertaste, and Dr. Shaw insisted Finch drink all of it. This strongly suggests a hidden substance within the tea, and Dr. Shaw's insistence points to a desired dosage.

story_elements.description: The Detective conducts a thorough, discreet search of the Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area, focusing on waste bins, discarded containers, or hidden stashes that might relate to Dr. Shaw's 'special blend' tea.
story_elements.reasoning: Dr. Shaw personally prepared the tea, and there's a chance she left behind evidence of unusual ingredients or specific preparations if she used a poison.

story_elements.description: The kitchen staff maintains strict cleanliness protocols, and the area was thoroughly cleaned after the incident. However, tucked away in a rarely used dry storage cabinet, the detective finds a small, unlabeled, empty bottle of a rare, imported herbal supplement, known for its calming properties... and in large doses, its potential to cause muscle weakness and paralysis. The bottle's label is in a foreign language, but a small, smudged sticker indicates it was ordered by "Dr. E. Shaw."
story_elements.new_info: An empty bottle of a rare, imported herbal supplement, capable of causing muscle weakness and paralysis in large doses, was found in a hidden cabinet in the kitchen. A smudged sticker links its order to 'Dr. E. Shaw'.

story_elements.description: The Detective attempts to research the rare, imported herbal supplement found in the kitchen, specifically looking into its origins, typical uses, and known distributors, to understand how Dr. Shaw might have acquired it.
story_elements.reasoning: Understanding the source and nature of the paralytic substance could provide a direct link to Dr. Shaw and her intent.

story_elements.description: The supplement is indeed rare, sourced from a remote, specialized clinic known for experimental holistic treatments in a country with lax regulations. The clinic's records are highly private, but a discreet inquiry reveals Dr. Shaw has been a regular, high-volume client for several months, often ordering 'research samples' for her 'personal studies.' This suggests premeditation.
story_elements.new_info: Dr. Shaw has been regularly ordering this rare, potentially paralytic supplement for months, indicating premeditation and a deeper, hidden agenda related to experimental substances.

story_elements.description: The Detective discreetly collects a sample of the shimmering residue from the medical bay shelf and arranges for an urgent off-site forensic analysis.
story_elements.reasoning: The residue is the only remaining physical trace of the mysterious vial Dr. Shaw removed. Analyzing it could provide definitive proof of its contents and link it to the paralytic found in the kitchen.

story_elements.description: The forensic lab reports a partial analysis, confirming the residue contains traces of a highly potent neurotoxin, chemically similar to the rare herbal supplement found. However, before a full identification can be made, the lab's equipment experiences a sudden, inexplicable malfunction, delaying further results and raising suspicions of sabotage.
story_elements.new_info: The residue confirms a potent neurotoxin, strongly linking it to the paralytic. The lab malfunction suggests active interference to obstruct the investigation.

story_elements.description: The Detective confronts Dr. Evelyn Shaw directly, presenting her with the empty bottle of the rare paralytic supplement found in the kitchen, and asking for an explanation regarding her repeated orders.
story_elements.reasoning: Directly challenging Dr. Shaw with the physical evidence of the paralytic substance and her suspicious ordering history will force a reaction, potentially revealing inconsistencies in her story or a confession under pressure.

story_elements.description: Dr. Shaw maintains her composure, claiming the supplement was for her personal 'holistic research' into natural sleep aids and muscle relaxants, which she sometimes experimented with herself, keeping it in the kitchen for convenience. She then subtly reminds the detective of the lodge's strict privacy policies and threatens to report harassment. However, her hands tremble slightly, and she avoids direct eye contact when explaining its 'personal use'.
story_elements.new_info: Dr. Shaw's composure cracks slightly under pressure, revealing physical signs of stress (trembling hands) despite her verbal denial and threats. Her explanation for the supplement's presence is flimsy and suspicious.

story_elements.description: The Detective discreetly searches Dr. Evelyn Shaw's private quarters, specifically looking for the unlabeled vial she claimed to have moved there, or any other evidence related to the paralytic.
story_elements.reasoning: Dr. Shaw's claim about moving the vial to her quarters was suspicious, and it's a prime location for hidden evidence. The previous search of the medical bay only yielded residue, not the vial itself.

story_elements.description: The vial is not present. However, hidden beneath a loose floorboard, the detective finds a small, waterproof pouch containing a burner phone and a handwritten ledger. The ledger details large, irregular payments to a private investigator for 'discretionary services' and mentions 'Finch's investigation' several times, along with references to 'old patient files' and 'suppressing information'. The burner phone shows recent calls to an untraceable number, and one outgoing message: 'Lab compromised. Proceed with contingency.'
story_elements.new_info: A hidden burner phone and ledger in Dr. Shaw's quarters reveal a paid private investigator, a direct link to 'Finch's investigation' and 'old patient files', and a message confirming the lab sabotage, indicating a wider, premeditated cover-up.

story_elements.description: The Detective confronts Dr. Evelyn Shaw again, this time presenting the evidence of the hidden ledger, the burner phone with the 'Lab compromised' message, and the details about the private investigator, demanding she explain the extent of her cover-up.
story_elements.reasoning: The overwhelming evidence of a premeditated cover-up, including obstruction of justice, should be enough to break Dr. Shaw's composure and force a confession or a significant revelation, especially regarding her motive of medical malpractice.

story_elements.description: Under intense pressure, Dr. Shaw's composure finally shatters. She confesses to hiring the private investigator to monitor Finch and to having Liam tamper with the lab, but she vehemently denies direct involvement in Finch's death, blaming a 'misunderstanding' of the paralytic's dosage by Liam, who she claims administered it without her direct instruction. She argues she only wanted to incapacitate Finch temporarily to retrieve his research, not kill him. However, her detailed knowledge of the paralytic's effects, her previous insistence that Finch drink *all* the tea, and her desperate attempt to shift blame to Liam, strongly implicate her as the mastermind behind the murder.
story_elements.new_info: Dr. Shaw confesses to hiring the PI and having Liam tamper with the lab, admitting to a cover-up. She attempts to blame Liam for administering the paralytic, claiming she only wanted to incapacitate Finch, but her deep knowledge of the substance and her desperate shifting of blame make her guilt evident.

plot_points.description: Professor Holt investigates the Medical Bay's inventory and Dr. Shaw's recent prescriptions, searching for any unusual or missing substances that could be a weapon.

Dr. Shaw's digital inventory logs are inaccessible due to a 'system update,' but Professor Holt notices a small, unlabeled vial tucked away in a back cabinet, separate from other medications, raising suspicion.
Professor Holt interviews Chloe, the room service attendant, about Arthur Finch's herbal tea delivery on the night of his death, focusing on its preparation and delivery.
plot_points.location_hint: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

plot_points.description: Chloe reveals that Dr. Shaw personally insisted on preparing and delivering a 'special blend' of herbal tea to Finch's room, bypassing standard procedures and appearing unusually focused on the task, which provided a direct opportunity for poisoning.
plot_points.location_hint: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

plot_points.description: Professor Holt attempts to discreetly retrieve the unlabeled vial from the Medical Bay for forensic analysis, recognizing its potential link to Finch's death given Dr. Shaw's involvement with the tea.

Dr. Shaw has already secured the unlabeled vial, claiming it was for aromatherapy and moved to her private quarters. However, Professor Holt notices a faint, shimmering chemical residue on the shelf where the vial sat, suggesting a substance inconsistent with essential oils.
Professor Holt reviews the lodge's limited security footage and interviews staff, seeking anyone who might have seen Dr. Shaw or other suspicious individuals near Finch's suite or the medical bay.

While security footage is unhelpful, a night porter recalls seeing Liam Shaw, Dr. Shaw's assistant, exiting Finch's suite (Room 307) late on the night of the murder, looking agitated, before heading towards the staff quarters. This contradicts Liam's general alibi of being in staff quarters all night.
Professor Holt re-interviews Chloe, pressing for specific details about the 'exotic herbs' Dr. Shaw used in Finch's tea, hoping she recognized them or recalled any unusual effects.
plot_points.location_hint: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

plot_points.description: Chloe remembers the tea had a faint, bitter aftertaste, which she initially dismissed. Crucially, she recalls Dr. Shaw's insistence that Finch drink *all* of the tea for 'maximum benefit,' strongly suggesting a hidden substance and a desired dosage.
plot_points.location_hint: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

plot_points.description: Professor Holt conducts a thorough, discreet search of the Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area, focusing on finding any discarded containers or hidden stashes related to Dr. Shaw's 'special blend' tea.
plot_points.location_hint: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

plot_points.description: Tucked away in a rarely used dry storage cabinet, Professor Holt discovers a small, unlabeled, empty bottle of a rare, imported herbal supplement. Its label, in a foreign language, is partially obscured, but a smudged sticker indicates it was ordered by 'Dr. E. Shaw.' The supplement is known for calming properties, but in large doses, it can cause muscle weakness and paralysis.
plot_points.location_hint: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

plot_points.description: Professor Holt researches the rare herbal supplement, investigating its origins, uses, and distributors to understand how Dr. Shaw might have acquired it.

Research reveals the supplement is rare, sourced from a specialized clinic with lax regulations. Discreet inquiries show Dr. Shaw has been a regular, high-volume client for months, ordering 'research samples' for 'personal studies,' strongly indicating premeditation.
Professor Holt discreetly collects a sample of the shimmering residue from the medical bay shelf and arranges for urgent off-site forensic analysis.

The forensic lab reports a partial analysis, confirming the residue contains traces of a highly potent neurotoxin chemically similar to the rare herbal supplement. However, before a full identification, the lab's equipment experiences a sudden, inexplicable malfunction, delaying results and raising suspicions of sabotage.
Professor Holt confronts Dr. Evelyn Shaw directly, presenting the empty bottle of the paralytic supplement found in the kitchen and demanding an explanation for her repeated orders.

Dr. Shaw maintains composure, claiming the supplement was for personal 'holistic research' into sleep aids, but her hands tremble slightly, and she avoids direct eye contact, betraying her stress despite her verbal denial and threats of harassment.
Professor Holt discreetly searches Dr. Evelyn Shaw's private quarters, looking for the unlabeled vial she claimed to have moved there, or any other hidden evidence.

The vial is not found, but beneath a loose floorboard, Professor Holt discovers a waterproof pouch containing a burner phone and a handwritten ledger. The ledger details payments to a private investigator, mentions 'Finch's investigation,' 'old patient files,' and 'suppressing information.' The burner phone shows a recent outgoing message: 'Lab compromised. Proceed with contingency.'

Professor Holt confronts Dr. Evelyn Shaw again, presenting the overwhelming evidence of the hidden ledger, the burner phone with the 'Lab compromised' message, and the details about the private investigator, demanding an explanation for the extent of her cover-up.

Under intense pressure, Dr. Shaw's composure shatters. She confesses to hiring the private investigator to monitor Finch and to having Liam tamper with the lab. She attempts to shift blame, claiming she only wanted to incapacitate Finch to retrieve his research and that Liam 'misunderstood' the paralytic's dosage, administering it without her direct instruction. However, her detailed knowledge of the substance, her prior insistence that Finch drink all the tea, and her desperate attempt to blame Liam strongly implicate her as the mastermind behind Finch's murder.

plot_holes_fixed: Liam Shaw's initial introduction as a 'suspicious presence' was vague; the night porter's sighting now explicitly introduces him and provides a specific suspicious action (leaving Finch's room agitated).

The implied prior knowledge of Liam's alibi was a plot hole; it's now assumed Professor Holt conducted initial interviews where Liam provided a general alibi, making the porter's statement a direct contradiction.

Several elements originally categorized as 'obstacles' were reclassified as 'clues' or 'events' to more accurately reflect their narrative function of revealing new information rather than solely impeding progress.
The 'new_info' fields from the raw elements were integrated directly into the 'description' field for conciseness and adherence to the schema requirements.

annotated_plot_points.description: Professor Holt investigates the Medical Bay's inventory and Dr. Shaw's recent prescriptions, searching for any unusual or missing substances that could be a weapon.

preconditions.description: Professor Holt must be in the Medical Bay to investigate.

annotated_plot_points.description: Dr. Shaw's digital inventory logs are inaccessible due to a 'system update,' but Professor Holt notices a small, unlabeled vial tucked away in a back cabinet, separate from other medications, raising suspicion.

preconditions.description: Plot point 001 must be completed to discover the vial.
Professor Holt must be in the Medical Bay.

effects.description: Professor Holt learns about the inaccessible digital inventory logs.
effects.value: Dr. Shaw's digital inventory logs are inaccessible.

effects.description: Professor Holt discovers a small, unlabeled vial.
effects.value: Discovered a small, unlabeled vial in the Medical Bay.

annotated_plot_points.description: Professor Holt interviews Chloe, the room service attendant, about Arthur Finch's herbal tea delivery on the night of his death, focusing on its preparation and delivery.
annotated_plot_points.location_hint: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

preconditions.description: Professor Holt must be in the Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area.
preconditions.value: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

preconditions.description: Chloe, the room service attendant, must be present.

annotated_plot_points.description: Chloe reveals that Dr. Shaw personally insisted on preparing and delivering a 'special blend' of herbal tea to Finch's room, bypassing standard procedures and appearing unusually focused on the task, which provided a direct opportunity for poisoning.
annotated_plot_points.location_hint: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

preconditions.description: Plot point 003 must be completed to get Chloe's revelation.

effects.description: Professor Holt learns that Dr. Shaw personally prepared and delivered Finch's tea.
effects.value: Dr. Shaw personally prepared and delivered a 'special blend' herbal tea to Finch.

annotated_plot_points.description: Professor Holt attempts to discreetly retrieve the unlabeled vial from the Medical Bay for forensic analysis, recognizing its potential link to Finch's death given Dr. Shaw's involvement with the tea.

preconditions.description: Plot point 002 must be completed to know about the vial.
Plot point 004 must be completed to link Shaw to the tea.
Professor Holt must be in the Medical Bay.

annotated_plot_points.description: Dr. Shaw has already secured the unlabeled vial, claiming it was for aromatherapy and moved to her private quarters. However, Professor Holt notices a faint, shimmering chemical residue on the shelf where the vial sat, suggesting a substance inconsistent with essential oils.

preconditions.description: Plot point 005 must be completed to trigger the discovery that the vial is gone.

effects.description: Professor Holt learns that Dr. Shaw moved the unlabeled vial to her private quarters.
effects.value: Dr. Shaw moved the unlabeled vial to her private quarters.

effects.description: Professor Holt discovers a faint, shimmering chemical residue on the shelf.
effects.value: Discovered faint, shimmering chemical residue on the Medical Bay shelf.

annotated_plot_points.description: Professor Holt reviews the lodge's limited security footage and interviews staff, seeking anyone who might have seen Dr. Shaw or other suspicious individuals near Finch's suite or the medical bay.

preconditions.description: Professor Holt must be in the Lodge Main Entrances and Lounge.
The Night Porter must be present for an interview.

annotated_plot_points.description: While security footage is unhelpful, a night porter recalls seeing Liam Shaw, Dr. Shaw's assistant, exiting Finch's suite (Room 307) late on the night of the murder, looking agitated, before heading towards the staff quarters. This contradicts Liam's general alibi of being in staff quarters all night.

preconditions.description: Plot point 007 must be completed for the night porter to recall the event.

effects.description: Professor Holt learns that Liam Shaw was seen exiting Finch's suite.
effects.value: Liam Shaw was seen exiting Finch's suite (Room 307) late on the night of the murder.

annotated_plot_points.description: Professor Holt re-interviews Chloe, pressing for specific details about the 'exotic herbs' Dr. Shaw used in Finch's tea, hoping she recognized them or recalled any unusual effects.
annotated_plot_points.location_hint: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

preconditions.description: Plot point 004 must be completed to know about the 'special blend' tea.
Professor Holt must be in the Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area.
preconditions.value: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

preconditions.description: Chloe, the room service attendant, must be present.

annotated_plot_points.description: Chloe remembers the tea had a faint, bitter aftertaste, which she initially dismissed. Crucially, she recalls Dr. Shaw's insistence that Finch drink *all* of the tea for 'maximum benefit,' strongly suggesting a hidden substance and a desired dosage.
annotated_plot_points.location_hint: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

preconditions.description: Plot point 009 must be completed to get Chloe's further recollections.

effects.description: Professor Holt learns about the tea's bitter aftertaste and Dr. Shaw's insistence on Finch drinking it all.
effects.value: The tea had a faint, bitter aftertaste, and Dr. Shaw insisted Finch drink all of it.

annotated_plot_points.description: Professor Holt conducts a thorough, discreet search of the Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area, focusing on finding any discarded containers or hidden stashes related to Dr. Shaw's 'special blend' tea.
annotated_plot_points.location_hint: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

preconditions.description: Plot point 004 must be completed to know about the tea.
Plot point 010 must be completed to have specific details about the tea.
Professor Holt must be in the Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area.
preconditions.value: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

annotated_plot_points.description: Tucked away in a rarely used dry storage cabinet, Professor Holt discovers a small, unlabeled, empty bottle of a rare, imported herbal supplement. Its label, in a foreign language, is partially obscured, but a smudged sticker indicates it was ordered by 'Dr. E. Shaw.' The supplement is known for calming properties, but in large doses, it can cause muscle weakness and paralysis.
annotated_plot_points.location_hint: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area

preconditions.description: Plot point 011 must be completed to find the bottle.

effects.description: Professor Holt discovers an empty bottle of a rare, imported herbal supplement.
effects.value: Discovered an empty bottle of a rare, imported herbal supplement, ordered by 'Dr. E. Shaw', in the kitchen.

annotated_plot_points.description: Professor Holt researches the rare herbal supplement, investigating its origins, uses, and distributors to understand how Dr. Shaw might have acquired it.

preconditions.description: Plot point 012 must be completed to have the supplement bottle to research.

annotated_plot_points.description: Research reveals the supplement is rare, sourced from a specialized clinic with lax regulations. Discreet inquiries show Dr. Shaw has been a regular, high-volume client for months, ordering 'research samples' for 'personal studies,' strongly indicating premeditation.

preconditions.description: Plot point 013 must be completed for the research to yield results.

effects.description: Professor Holt learns about Dr. Shaw's regular, high-volume orders of the supplement.
effects.value: Dr. Shaw has been a regular, high-volume client for months, ordering the rare herbal supplement.

annotated_plot_points.description: Professor Holt discreetly collects a sample of the shimmering residue from the medical bay shelf and arranges for urgent off-site forensic analysis.

preconditions.description: Plot point 006 must be completed to know about the shimmering residue.
Professor Holt must be in the Medical Bay.

annotated_plot_points.description: The forensic lab reports a partial analysis, confirming the residue contains traces of a highly potent neurotoxin chemically similar to the rare herbal supplement. However, before a full identification, the lab's equipment experiences a sudden, inexplicable malfunction, delaying results and raising suspicions of sabotage.

preconditions.description: Plot point 015 must be completed for the forensic analysis to begin.

effects.description: Professor Holt learns that the residue contains traces of a potent neurotoxin, and the lab equipment malfunctioned.
effects.value: Forensic analysis confirms neurotoxin in residue; lab equipment malfunctioned.

annotated_plot_points.description: Professor Holt confronts Dr. Evelyn Shaw directly, presenting the empty bottle of the paralytic supplement found in the kitchen and demanding an explanation for her repeated orders.

preconditions.description: Plot point 012 must be completed to have the empty bottle.
Professor Holt must be in Dr. Evelyn Shaw's Office.
Dr. Evelyn Shaw must be present for the confrontation.

annotated_plot_points.description: Dr. Shaw maintains composure, claiming the supplement was for personal 'holistic research' into sleep aids, but her hands tremble slightly, and she avoids direct eye contact, betraying her stress despite her verbal denial and threats of harassment.

preconditions.description: Plot point 017 must be completed for Dr. Shaw to react.

effects.description: Professor Holt observes Dr. Shaw's physical tells and verbal denial.
effects.value: Dr. Shaw's hands tremble despite her denial about the supplement.

annotated_plot_points.description: Professor Holt discreetly searches Dr. Evelyn Shaw's private quarters, looking for the unlabeled vial she claimed to have moved there, or any other hidden evidence.

preconditions.description: Plot point 006 must be completed to know the vial was moved to her quarters.
Professor Holt must be in Dr. Evelyn Shaw's Private Quarters.

annotated_plot_points.description: The vial is not found, but beneath a loose floorboard, Professor Holt discovers a waterproof pouch containing a burner phone and a handwritten ledger. The ledger details payments to a private investigator, mentions 'Finch's investigation,' 'old patient files,' and 'suppressing information.' The burner phone shows a recent outgoing message: 'Lab compromised. Proceed with contingency.'

preconditions.description: Plot point 019 must be completed for the search to yield results.

effects.description: Professor Holt discovers a burner phone and a handwritten ledger in Dr. Shaw's quarters.
effects.value: Discovered a burner phone and a ledger detailing payments and 'Lab compromised' message in Dr. Shaw's quarters.

annotated_plot_points.description: Professor Holt confronts Dr. Evelyn Shaw again, presenting the overwhelming evidence of the hidden ledger, the burner phone with the 'Lab compromised' message, and the details about the private investigator, demanding an explanation for the extent of her cover-up.

preconditions.description: Plot point 018 must be completed to have had the initial confrontation.
Plot point 020 must be completed to have the burner phone and ledger.
Professor Holt must be in Dr. Evelyn Shaw's Office.
Dr. Evelyn Shaw must be present for the confrontation.

annotated_plot_points.description: Under intense pressure, Dr. Shaw's composure shatters. She confesses to hiring the private investigator to monitor Finch and to having Liam tamper with the lab. She attempts to shift blame, claiming she only wanted to incapacitate Finch to retrieve his research and that Liam 'misunderstood' the paralytic's dosage, administering it without her direct instruction. However, her detailed knowledge of the substance, her prior insistence that Finch drink all the tea, and her desperate attempt to blame Liam strongly implicate her as the mastermind behind Finch's murder.

preconditions.description: Plot point 021 must be completed to trigger Dr. Shaw's confession.

effects.description: Professor Holt learns of Dr. Shaw's full confession and her role as the mastermind.
effects.value: Dr. Shaw confesses to orchestrating Finch's murder and attempts to blame Liam Shaw.

protected_variables.description: Knowledge of the small, unlabeled vial discovered in the Medical Bay.
The fact that Dr. Shaw personally prepared and delivered Finch's tea.
The discovery of the faint, shimmering chemical residue on the Medical Bay shelf.
The fact that Liam Shaw was seen exiting Finch's suite.
The fact that the tea had a bitter aftertaste and Dr. Shaw insisted Finch drink all of it.
The discovery of the empty bottle of paralytic herbal supplement.
The fact of Dr. Shaw's regular, high-volume orders of the supplement, indicating premeditation.
The fact that the medical bay residue contained traces of neurotoxin.
The discovery of the burner phone and handwritten ledger in Dr. Shaw's quarters.
Dr. Evelyn Shaw's physical integrity and ability to confess.

causal_spans.condition_description: A small, unlabeled vial exists in the Medical Bay
Dr. Shaw personally prepared and delivered Finch's 'special blend' herbal tea
The unlabeled vial is in Dr. Shaw's private quarters
Faint, shimmering chemical residue exists on the Medical Bay shelf
Liam Shaw was seen exiting Finch's suite (Room 307) late on the night of the murder
The tea had a faint, bitter aftertaste and Dr. Shaw insisted Finch drink all of it
An empty bottle of a rare, imported herbal supplement, ordered by 'Dr. E. Shaw', exists in the kitchen
Dr. Shaw has been a regular, high-volume client for months, ordering the rare herbal supplement
Forensic analysis confirms neurotoxin in residue
Lab equipment malfunctioned during forensic analysis
Dr. Shaw maintains her denial about the supplement, but shows physical tells of stress
A burner phone and handwritten ledger exist in Dr. Shaw's quarters
The burner phone contains a 'Lab compromised' message

rooms.name: Main Lounge
rooms.description: The heart of Peak Vista Lodge, a grand space with a roaring fireplace and panoramic windows showcasing the blizzard outside. Plush armchairs and tasteful decor offer a facade of comfort, but an undercurrent of tension lingers in the air.

rooms.name: West Wing Corridor
rooms.description: A long, carpeted hallway stretching into the lodge's western reaches. The silence here is only broken by the distant howl of the wind and the faint hum of unseen machinery. Doors line both sides, leading to various facilities.

rooms.name: Medical Bay
rooms.description: A small, well-equipped medical facility, surprisingly modern for a remote lodge. Sterile white walls and the scent of antiseptic contrast sharply with the cozy lodge aesthetic. A single examination bed and various medical instruments are neatly arranged.

rooms.name: Forensic Lab
rooms.description: A makeshift forensic lab, hastily set up in what appears to be a former storage room. Specialized equipment, including microscopes and chemical reagents, glints under harsh fluorescent lights. A chilling sense of scientific inquiry pervades the space.

rooms.name: Dr. Evelyn Shaw's Office
rooms.description: Dr. Shaw's office, a blend of professional order and personal clutter. Medical texts share shelf space with framed photos, and a large desk is strewn with papers, hinting at a busy and perhaps troubled mind. The air is thick with the scent of old books and herbal tea.

rooms.name: East Wing Corridor
rooms.description: This corridor leads towards the lodge's service areas. The plush carpet gives way to more utilitarian flooring, and the sounds of the main lounge fade, replaced by a faint clatter and hum from beyond a heavy service door. A back staircase is visible at one end.

rooms.name: Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area
rooms.description: The bustling heart of the lodge's culinary operations, now eerily quiet. Stainless steel surfaces gleam under harsh lights, and the lingering scent of spices and baked goods hangs in the air. Industrial-sized appliances stand ready, but no chefs are in sight.

rooms.name: Library
rooms.description: A cozy, wood-paneled library filled with an impressive collection of books. A grand fireplace, currently unlit, dominates one wall, and comfortable leather armchairs invite quiet contemplation. The silence here is profound, broken only by the gentle creak of the blizzard outside.

rooms.name: Grand Staircase Lobby
rooms.description: A dramatic, open space dominated by a sweeping grand staircase that ascends to the upper floors. Ornate railings and a crystal chandelier speak of old-world luxury. The air here feels colder, perhaps due to the height of the ceiling or the drafts from the exterior doors.

rooms.name: Third Floor Landing
rooms.description: The landing at the top of the grand staircase, offering a view down to the Main Lounge. A plush carpet muffles footsteps, and the corridor ahead leads deeper into the guest suites. The blizzard's muffled roar is a constant, distant companion.

rooms.name: Third Floor Corridor
rooms.description: A quiet, well-appointed corridor on the third floor, lined with doors to guest suites. Each door is heavy and unmarked, lending an air of privacy and mystery to the residents within. The silence here is almost unnerving.

rooms.name: Arthur Finch's Suite (Room 307)
rooms.description: A lavish, oversized guest suite, currently in disarray. Expensive furniture is askew, and papers are scattered across a desk, suggesting a hurried departure or a struggle. The large windows offer a dizzying view of the snow-covered mountains, now obscured by the blizzard.

rooms.name: Dr. Evelyn Shaw's Private Quarters
rooms.description: Dr. Shaw's personal sanctuary, surprisingly sparse yet meticulously organized. A single bed, a small writing desk, and a few personal effects suggest a life dedicated to work rather than comfort. A faint, medicinal scent lingers.

objects.name: Dr. Shaw's Prescription Log
objects.description: A logbook detailing Dr. Shaw's recent medical prescriptions and inventory usage. It seems to have undergone a 'system update' making recent entries inaccessible.

objects.name: Unlabeled Vial
objects.description: A small, unlabeled vial tucked away in a back cabinet of the Medical Bay. Its contents are unknown.

objects.name: Arthur Finch's Herbal Tea
objects.description: The herbal tea delivered to Arthur Finch, notable for its faint, bitter aftertaste. Dr. Shaw personally insisted Finch drink all of it.

objects.name: Shimmering Chemical Residue
objects.description: A faint, shimmering chemical residue on a shelf in the Medical Bay, where the unlabeled vial once sat. It suggests a substance inconsistent with essential oils.

objects.name: Lodge Security Footage
objects.description: Recordings from the lodge's limited security cameras, covering main entrances and lounge areas. The footage is unhelpful for direct identification of suspects near Finch's suite.

objects.name: Empty Herbal Supplement Bottle
objects.description: A small, unlabeled, empty bottle of a rare, imported herbal supplement, found hidden in a dry storage cabinet. A smudged sticker indicates it was ordered by 'Dr. E. Shaw'.

objects.name: Forensic Lab Equipment
objects.description: Specialized equipment in an off-site forensic lab. It experienced a sudden, inexplicable malfunction during analysis of the residue, delaying results.

objects.name: Burner Phone and Handwritten Ledger
objects.description: A waterproof pouch containing a burner phone and a handwritten ledger. The ledger details payments to a private investigator, mentions 'Finch's investigation,' 'old patient files,' and 'suppressing information.' The phone shows a recent outgoing message: 'Lab compromised. Proceed with contingency.'

objects.name: Dr. Shaw's shoe
objects.description: One of Dr. Evelyn Shaw's shoes, bearing a distinct scratch or scuff mark on the side.

action_rules.description: Talk to Dr. Evelyn Shaw about what she knows regarding the situation.

preconditions.description: The player must be in the Medical Bay.
Dr. Evelyn Shaw must be present.

action_rules.effects_description: Dr. Evelyn Shaw will reveal some crucial information about the events at Peak Vista Lodge, potentially providing a new lead or a new fact to the player.

action_rules.description: Examine Dr. Evelyn Shaw's shoe for a scratch or other signs of a struggle.

preconditions.description: The player must be in the Medical Bay.
Dr. Evelyn Shaw must be present in the Medical Bay.
Dr. Shaw's shoe must be accessible for examination.

action_rules.effects_description: The player closely examines Dr. Evelyn Shaw's shoe and confirms the presence of a distinct scuff mark or damage, suggesting it was involved in a struggle or altercation. This reveals the fact 'dr_shaw_shoe_scratched'.

action_rules.description: Examine the empty herbal supplement bottle and Arthur Finch's Herbal Tea.

preconditions.description: The player must be in the Lodge Kitchen and Room Service Prep Area.
The Empty Herbal Supplement Bottle must be present in the current location.
Arthur Finch's Herbal Tea must be present in the current location.

action_rules.effects_description: The player closely examines the Empty Herbal Supplement Bottle and Arthur Finch's Herbal Tea. This reveals details about their labels, ingredients, and potential connections, leading to the fact 'bottle_and_tea_details_revealed'.
